title: Post by Pari Natarajan
description:
AI added $350B in market cap. SaaS and SIs lost $500B
...
We looked at the market cap of companies across SIs, hyperscalers, SaaS companies, as well as AI-native companies. Earlier in the decade, software and AI were clustered together. But now, they are poles apart.
...
When Claude Code was released last year, you see that the AI companies' market cap has gone up 140%, versus the GSI and SaaS companies, which have gone down 25%. So there is a net increase of USD 350 billion in market cap for AI-native and hyperscalers, and a net reduction of USD 500 billion for the SIs and SaaS companies. Market cap gives you a view of what the market is predicting to happen in the future.
...
And now, this year, we are going to have three trillion-dollar IPOs. SpaceX is already public and the two AI labs are slated to go public this year. The money to fund these has to come from somewhere and might come from the the SaaS and SI ecosystem.
...
> Sharp framing, Pari. The

title: Post by Ivan Fioravanti
description:
# Post by Ivan Fioravanti · LinkedIn · 2026-07-08

**Ivan Fioravanti**: Founder at Stealth Local AI Startup with 26 years 6 months of experience. Previous roles include CTO Co-Founder and Managing Director at CoreView, CTO and co-founder at 4ward365 at CoreView, and Board Member at SmartFlow 365 at SmartFlow365. Based in Milan, Lombardy, Italy [IT].

---

Apple Silicon Power unleashed with a custom Metal Kernel! 🚀

I tested MTPLX v2 with QWEN 3.6 27B and compared it with oMLX on M5 Max and DGX Spark on vllm using nvfp4 model version.

I've reached 82.8 tps of max decoding speed on Apple Silicon! 🔥

Custom Metal Kernel design specifically for this model and for M5 Max is just perfect! This is the way forward!

Here is a website with the recap, built with GLM 5.2 running locally 💪 on M3 Ultra: https://lnkd.in/dDAKjaMd

title: Databricks Genie Pricing: The Shift to Token-Based Pricing
description:
There have been a handful of articles and posts criticizing Databricks' announcement that Genie One, Genie Agents, and Genie Code users will receive a monthly allowance of token credits and then pay, at cost, for any additional LLM tokens they use.
...
The criticism is understandable, but I think the change deserves a more nuanced perspective. Databricks is not eliminating free usage. Customers will still receive a monthly allowance, but they will now have a clearer and more predictable way to continue using these products after that allowance is exhausted.
...
change is also happening as the Genie products themselves become significantly more capable. My view is that the pricing model is catching up with the products: giving customers more transparency, giving Databricks more flexibility to
...

for enterprises to scale these capabilities.

title: Q1 2026 AI Agent Startup Consolidation
description: A massive consolidation event occurred in the AI agent sector in Q1 2026, with approximately 1,800 AI startups shutting down (increasing 2.6x from Q1 2025). Notable losses included Jasper (sold for parts) and Copy.ai (merged).

title: Gemini Spark Goes Desktop: macOS, Connected Apps, and Real-Time Monitoring
description:
Google's always-on AI agent lands on macOS with desktop file automation, third-party app integrations (Canva, Dropbox, Instacart), MCP support, and trigger-based event monitoring — all available today for AI Ultra subscribers.
...
its I/O 20
...
1. macOS app (Beta) — Spark now runs natively on your Mac, automating files and apps inside your desktop environment, with remote task execution from your phone coming soon
2. Connected apps + MCP — Google Keep, Tasks, Canva, Dropbox, Instacart, OpenTable, Zillow Rentals, plus custom Model Context Protocol support
3. Real-time topic tracking — Spark monitors blogs, news, social media, finance, weather, sports, and email, sending push notifications when triggers fire
...
Spark on macOS is not the Gemini web app in a desktop wrapper. It runs as a persistent background agent with file-level access (user-permissioned) and can:
...
The killer feature in the pipeline is remote task execution. You’ll be able to assign a multi-step task from your phone

title: Claude Private VPC Deployment
description: Anthropic enabled private VPC deployments for Claude via AWS PrivateLink and GCP Private Service Connect for enterprises demanding no public egress.

title: SaaS Meter | Golub Growth
description:
Q1’26 was marked by a meaningful pullback in public SaaS valuations. The Golub Capital Public SaaS Tracker declined ~28% since Q1’25, compared to a ~16% gain for the broader S&P 500 over the same period. Every SaaS vertical experienced meaningful multiple compression, with Security seeing the largest absolute contraction (down ~2.8x to ~8.1x).
...
Valuations declined across all growth buckets. The 20–30% growth cohort fell from ~11.2x to ~6.0x, while 40–50% growers declined from ~14.3x to ~7.6x QoQ. Even >50% growth companies saw multiples compress to ~7.8x, down from ~14.8x in Q3’25.
...
Higher-multiple companies showed improved sales efficiency, with sales and marketing spend ~44.6% of revenue in the 10x+ cohort in Q1’26, down from ~59% in Q4’25—indicating a shift toward profitability and disciplined growth.

title: Belgian DPA Publishes Findings on AI Chatbot GDPR Compliance
description: In findings published in a July 2026 report, the Belgian Supervisory Authority (APD) highlighted that AI chatbot developers and operators prioritized development, user experience and scalability over GDPR compliance, citing issues with sensitive data in free-text interactions, data minimization and sharing with external providers.

title: ChatGPT Workspace Agents Now Bill Credits on Top of Seats: The July 6 Cutover Math (2026) | UsageBox
description: TL;DR (July 2026): On July 6, 2026 the free preview of ChatGPT Workspace Agents ended. Every agent run inside a Business, Enterprise, or Edu workspace now draws down workspace credits on top of the per-seat fee you already pay. The published GPT-5.5 rates: 125 credits per million input tokens, 12.50 per million cached input tokens, 750 per million output tokens - a typical end-to-end run lands around 5 to 25 credits. What OpenAI did not publish is a consumption table for real workflows, so every team that automated prospecting, reporting, or ticket routing during the free spring now has a variable operating cost it cannot budget from the vendor's own docs. The seat is the floor; the meter is the bill. If you want a forecast, you have to build it from your own per-run usage data - nobody else has it.\n...\nThe July 6 cutover is the clearest single example of the pricing shift that has been rolling through AI software all year: the subscription seat stops being the price and becomes the en

title: EU Court Upholds €4.1 Billion Google Android Antitrust Fine
description: The EU Court of Justice upheld the European Commission's 2018 €4.1 billion fine against Google for Android antitrust practices involving bundling and defaults.

title: SaaS Revenue Multiples June 2026: Trends and Insights\n\nWhat’s the median revenue multiple for publicly traded SaaS compa
description: SaaS Revenue Multiples June 2026: Trends and Insights What’s the median revenue multiple for publicly traded SaaS companies, how has it evolved, and why? As of June 30th, 2026, the median revenue multiple was 3,93x, which is: 👉 35% lower YoY (6,06x at the end of June 2025), but rebounded 20% from the record-level low of 3,27x in the middle of April 2026 👉 79% lower from all-time high (19.04x on Dec 18, 2020) 💡 Why the drop since 2020? It’s all about slower growth. The median YoY revenue growth rate for SaaS companies is now 20%, near the lowest levels since 2014 and far from the +30% YoY long-term growth in the last two decades until the end of 2022, which fueled higher multiples in previous years. ⚖️ Key takeaway: Slower growth = lower multiples. So, unless revenue growth will significantly increase, there is no coming back to valuations (revenue multiples) seen during the Tech Bubble (H2-2020 to H1 2022). But in the last months, the key driver that accelerated SaaS sell-off are most

title: EDPB Adopts Draft Guidelines on Web Scraping for Generative AI
description: On 8 July 2026, the European Data Protection Board adopted draft Guidelines 03/2026 on web scraping in the context of generative AI for public consultation until 30 October 2026, clarifying GDPR legal bases, conditions for processing special categories of data, and mitigating measures including data minimization, exclusion of sensitive sources, prompt deletion or anonymization, and safeguards against model memorization.

title: Jamf Launches Native AI Control Plane for Mac
description: Jamf announced general availability of AI Governance within Jamf for Mac for visibility into AI tools, policy enforcement, and audit-ready reports on enterprise Mac devices.

title: The Token Economy - State of AI • MID-2026 • A SPECIAL REPORT
description:
If you want to know whether the AI agent — the software that doesn't just answer but acts — is real, do not read the keynotes. Read the meters. The most persuasive economic document of 2026 is the token counter at Fireworks AI, a San Francisco inference platform founded by Lin Qiao, the engineer who led PyTorch at Meta. Fireworks serves roughly 30 trillion tokens a day for more than 10,000 customers — Cursor, Uber, Samsung, Notion, Shopify among them — and its annualized revenue, an estimated $305 million at the end of 2025, had reportedly reached $800 million by May. Investors responded the way they do: reports put a new round in the works at around $15 billion, nearly quadruple the company's October 2025 valuation.

title: "We Buy Failing Apps" — The Digital Junk Dealer That Made a Crazy Nasdaq Debut With a $40 Trillion Market Cap - MK
description:
Bending Spoonsacquired Evernote, Vimeo, WeTransfer, and Eventbrite one after another, then slashed bloated costs and completely redesigned product development and operations to fit its shared platform system.It did so.
...
Among Bending Spoons' many "repair jobs," the market's masterpiece is undoubtedly the acquisition of Evernote in late 2022.
...
As trendy collaboration tools such as Notion rose rapidly and Evernote's chronic heaviness and software bugs were left unresolved, the app slowly sank. Millions of users kept using it month after month for years, but it posted huge losses every year.
...
Right after the acquisition, Bending Spoons completely overhauled the heavy software architecture. It broke apart a system that had been left untouched for more than a decade and rebuilt the virtual servers with a modern setup.
...
In the first year alone, the company carried out more than 75 major feature improvements. As the app's chronic bugs and slow performance were fixed, stability imp

title: Emergence of AI-First HR SaaS Category
description: Industry reports from Q2 2026 identify AI agents and copilots for recruiting, learning, and scheduling as the fastest-emerging category in HR SaaS, driving a shift toward AI-first enterprise valuations.
